title: Get In Touch - John Healey MP
meta_description:
Every year thousands of people contact me with individual issues through my constituency office in Wath-upon-Dearne, which is open full time, or by speaking to me at one of the regular advice surgeries and coffee mornings I hold around the constituency.

People ask me for assistance with local community problems, tax credits, pensions, benefits, matters involving
text_excerpt: Get In Touch - John Healey MP Skip to content Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Get In Touch You are here: Home Get In Touch Get In Touch 2025-11-18T15:26:36+00:00 Every year thousands of people contact me with individual issues through my constituency office in Wath-upon-Dearne, which is open full time, or by speaking to me at one of the regular advice surgeries and coffee mornings I hold around the constituency. People ask me for assistance with local community problems, tax credits, pensions, benefits, matters involving the Child Support Agency and government policies and legislation, and many more issues. For more information on what MPs can and cannot do, visit the ‘Help and advice ’ page. I am always ready to listen and will do my best to help – so please get in touch should you need me at any time by completing the form below. Parliamentary rules mean I can only take up your case if you live in the Rawmarsh and Conisbrough constituency. If you’re not sure, you can check at this website . You can email me at john.healey.mp@parliament.uk . Please write or call in to: John Healey MP 79 High Street Wath Upon Dearne Rotherham S63 7QB Constituency office phone number: 01709 875943 For Ministry of Defence queries, please call 0207 218 9000 Please do not call the constituency office with defence related queries. John’s Team Holly Williamson – Head of Constituency Phone: 01709 512463 Email: holly.williamson@parliament.uk ______________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Greg Kuczmaida – Diary Manager & Caseworker Phone: 01709 875943 Email: greg.kuczmaida@parliament.uk ______________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Emily Walker – Communications Officer (Constituency) Phone: 01709 512463 Email: emily.walker@parliament.uk Nieve Elliott – Caseworker Phone: 01709 875943 Email: nieve.elliott@parliament.uk ______________________________________________________________________________________________________________ John Williams – Caseworker (part-time) Phone: 01709 875943 Email: johnb.williams@parliament.uk ______________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Jackie Bate – Office Manager Phone: 0207 219 5170 Email: batej@parliament.uk Privacy Policy I am the MP for Rawmarsh and Conisbrough Constituency. As your MP, it is important that I and my office can keep in touch with constituents about my work, take up casework on their behalf and ask for views on local issues. As a Labour MP I also have access to other information which I or my office, or volunteers working with me, will use for campaigning purposes or for communicating with Labour Party members. In respect of that information, the Labour Party is the data controller, and you should consult their privacy policy (https://labour.org.uk/privacy-policy/ ) for details of what information they hold, why, and how they use and process that information. This page explains how I collect and use personal data, the legal basis for doing so and provides information about your rights in respect of your personal data for which I am the data controller. My responsible officer for data protection can be contacted at emily.walker@parliament.uk or on 01709 875943. Data collection and use The office of John Healey MP will only collect and use personal information for the specific purpose for which it has been obtained. I will undertake casework using personal information provided by or on behalf of a constituent. It may be necessary for me to share your information with third party organisations. I will only do where it is necessary and reasonable to do
